metadata.description: Community-maintained MLB National League injury database

injuries.status: Rehabbing — late 2025 return possible, no definitive date set
injuries.details: Tore left ACL near end of May 2024 while running the bases against the Pittsburgh Pirates — his second major knee injury (tore right ACL in July 2021). Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 4, 2024. By February 2025, reported at 90-95% in running and cutting with no restrictions. Expected to miss the first month of the 2025 season. Braves GM Alex Anthopoulos: 'We think getting him back is going to be big.' Will need a Minor League rehab stint once cleared for cutting and outfield play. Managers cautious after lasting discomfort plagued his 2022 return from the first ACL tear. As of July 2025, Braves manager Walt Weiss stated Acuña is 'a long way' from returning.
injuries.previous_injury_note: Previously tore right ACL on July 10, 2021; returned late April 2022 but dealt with lingering discomfort all season.

injuries.details: Reported hamstring discomfort in early July 2025. Status and timeline uncertain; managers described Acuña as still 'a long way' from returning as of late July 2025.

injuries.status: Recovered — lingering performance effects resolved by September
injuries.details: An undiagnosed stomach illness caused Betts to lose approximately 20 pounds in two weeks during spring 2025. He described feeling like his body was 'eating itself.' Missed the season-opening Tokyo Series. Returned on March 26, 2025 at ~157 lbs instead of his customary ~175 lbs. After a strong first 15 games (.304/.400/.554 with 4 HRs), he struggled to a .637 OPS over his next 91 games. By early August he declared 'my season's kind of over.' Subsequently shifted mindset to a 'supporting cast' role. Bounced back over the final 47 games, batting .317 and nearly doubling his home run total (11 to 20). In the Wild Card sweep of the Reds, he posted 6 hits including 3 doubles. Finished the season at .258/.732 OPS. Also dealt with the death of his stepfather during the season, compounding the physical and mental toll.
injuries.personal_note: Betts described going through phases of anger, sadness, and eventual acceptance regarding both the illness and his season struggles.

injuries.details: Removed in the first inning of a game versus the Nationals on August 4, 2025, due to lower back pain. Walked to first base and scored on Freeman's double, but was replaced by Miguel Rojas at shortstop when the Dodgers took the field. Expected to be evaluated on a game-by-game basis.

injuries.details: Sprained right ankle on Sept 25, 2024 in the final week of the regular season; missed last 3 regular-season games but played through the injury during the entire 2024 postseason, including the World Series where he hit a walk-off grand slam in Game 1 and earned World Series MVP honors. Underwent surgery in early December 2024 — debridement and removal of loose bodies from the right ankle. Surgeons also found torn ligaments but those had healed enough that they did not need surgical repair. Freeman called it 'quite a nasty injury.' Re-injured the ankle slipping in the shower at home on March 31, 2025 ('freak accident'), leading to a 10-day IL stint (retroactive to March 31, eligible to return April 10). Returned feeling 'the best I've felt since I got hurt.' By mid-season 2025: hitting .376 with 1.171 OPS through 30 games — 40 points above his previous career best through 30 games. Undergoes ~1.5 hours of daily ankle treatment before and after every game, uses heel lifts and tape. Expected to continue treatment until at least the All-Star break. Still not 100% but feeling good enough not to think about it on the field.
injuries.rehab_note: Used a boost treadmill (body-weight de-loading) during early rehab. Also dealt with a torn rib muscle (left side) that caused him to miss the Tokyo Series in mid-March 2025. Scar tissue buildup in the rib kept him out of the Tokyo Series. Rib cartilage broken during 2024 postseason was fought through to earn WS MVP.

injuries.details: Left wrist contusion from being hit by an 88 mph sinker from Jose Quintana on July 20, 2025. X-rays negative. Described as day-to-day. 'Dodged a bullet' per manager Roberts. If Freeman were forced to miss time, Dalton Rushing would start at first base.

sources.title: Dodgers' Dave Roberts Provides Freddie Freeman Injury Update

injuries.status: Active — returned quickly, no setbacks expected
injuries.details: Exited a game on August 27, 2025 with a neck stinger radiating into his right shoulder. Returned to the lineup two days later. Manager Dave Roberts: 'There's still some kind of lingering effects, but nothing compromising.' Dodgers used the off day to keep him comfortable. Not expected to miss significant time.
